title: Cat Refusing to Eat Wet Food for 24 Hours: Triage Guide
meta_desc: Is your cat skipping meals? Learn why a 24-hour fast is a medium urgency concern and how to triage your cat's health at home.
content_html:
TL;DR: If your cat has refused food for 24 hours, contact your veterinarian within 48 hours to prevent metabolic complications; seek immediate care if they also show symptoms like lethargy or jaundice.

What should I know if my cat stops eating?

  • When a cat stops eating for 24 hours, it is medically known as inappetence or anorexia.
  • Because cats have a unique metabolism, even a short period without calories can lead to secondary health complications.
  • It is important to monitor whether they are refusing all food or just a specific texture to help differentiate between pickiness and a medical issue.

How urgent is it if my cat hasn't eaten for 24 hours?

  • A cat refusing food for 24 hours is classified as a medium urgency situation.
  • You should contact your veterinarian for an appointment within the next 24 to 48 hours.
  • The urgency increases to high if the lack of appetite is accompanied by vomiting, diarrhea, extreme lethargy, or yellowing of the eyes.

How can a photo or video help my veterinarian triage my cat's loss of appetite?

  • A clear photo of your cat's face and mouth can reveal swelling or dental issues.
  • A close-up of their gums (which should be bubblegum pink) can help the vet check for anemia or jaundice.
  • A video of their posture can indicate if they are in pain.

Clinical Context (Merck Veterinary Manual)

If a cat refuses to eat wet food for 24 hours, offering a palatable, odiferous food initially is recommended. If the cat salivates or objects, all food should be removed to avoid inducing a food aversion. If oral feeding is not tolerated, cautiously initiate feeding a liquid diet with supplements via a nasoesophageal tube, starting with a small volume of tepid water (5-10 mL) to assess tolerance. After a few days of nasoesophageal feeding, consider placing an esophagostomy tube if the cat is a reasonable anesthetic risk, ensuring the distal tip is 2-4 cm cranial to the esophageal-gastric junction, documented with a lateral thoracic radiograph.

Chapter: Nutrition, Gastroenterology

Source: The Merck Veterinary Manual, 11th Edition (Page 458)

faqs.question: Can I wait another day to see if they eat?
faqs.answer: It is generally not recommended to wait beyond 24 hours. Cats are prone to Hepatic Lipidosis (Fatty Liver Disease) when they stop eating, which can become life-threatening very quickly.

faqs.question: Should I try offering different foods?
faqs.answer: Yes, you can try offering a small amount of plain boiled chicken or a different flavor of wet food to see if they have an appetite, but if they refuse all options, seek veterinary advice.

faqs.question: What are common causes for sudden food refusal?
faqs.answer: Common causes include dental pain, nausea from kidney or liver issues, respiratory infections that block their sense of smell, or stress-related anxiety.
